rationale: The previous generation catastrophically overcorrected: shaping at 15x produced 7691 per episode, completely dominating all other signals and causing a 7660 generated-private gap. The agent learned to hover near the pad indefinitely (901 steps) without ever landing. To fix this: (1) reduce shaping to 3x (gentle guidance, ~20-40 per episode), (2) increase velocity penalty to -0.5 to discourage excessive speed, (3) increase angle penalty to -0.5 to encourage stable orientation, (4) add a small per-step survival penalty of -0.1 to create urgency, (5) increase terminal bonus to 500 and tighten landing tolerances (position 0.15, velocity 0.3, angle 0.2) to make landing the dominant reward. This targets a balanced composition: shaping ~30, velocity -20, angle -10, survival -90, terminal 500.

components.description: Gentle shaping reward for proximity to pad (3x multiplier, faster decay).
Moderate penalty on horizontal and vertical velocity to encourage controlled descent.
Penalize large angle and angular velocity.
Fuel penalty removed to avoid discouraging necessary engine use.

components.expression: 500.0 * float(done and obs[6] > 0.5 and obs[7] > 0.5 and abs(obs[0]) < 0.15 and abs(obs[1]) < 0.15 and abs(obs[2]) < 0.3 and abs(obs[3]) < 0.3 and abs(obs[4]) < 0.2)
components.description: Large terminal bonus for successful landing with moderate tolerances.
